config.systemPrompt:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

methodology: One read-only external-project analysis task executed through a DeepSeek LLM agent; it is operationally measured but not success-scored.

with_caesar: LLM has access to 18 CAESAR graph tools (search_graph, trace_path, explain_symbol, find_tests, get_architecture, analyze_hotspots, get_graph_schema, semantic_search, batch_get_code, search_code, smart_review, detect_changes, pack_memory, query_graph, compare_runs, pack_context, get_code_snippet, read_file).
without_caesar: LLM has access to read_file + grep only.
Fresh conversation per task x condition x run. No shared history or cache between conditions.
Paid microbenchmark protocol: exact deepseek-v4-flash, temperature 0, fixed per-round seeds, identical prompts/order/limits/runtime; no model or provider fallback.
Mode: live API calls. Suite: external-project.
Cost is estimated from token usage and configured pricing. Wall time includes API latency.
ROI claims blocked when baseline invalid or sample size too small.

messages.content:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

Give me a scalability health check of freqtrade. Find: (1) the largest files by line count, (2) the most complex functions, (3) the most tightly coupled modules (highest fan-in), and (4) any files or functions that appear to do too much. Use whatever graph or analysis tools are available. Do NOT read every file — use structural queries. Respond with JSON: {"largest_files":[{"file":"...","lines":N}], "most_complex":[{"name":"...","file":"...","complexity":N}], "tightest_coupling":[{"name":"...","file":"...","fan_in":N}], "god_objects":[{"name":"...","file":"...","why":"..."}], "methodology":"...", "summary":"..."}.

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments: {"query": "MATCH (f:Function) WHERE f.complexity IS NOT NULL RETURN f.name AS name, f.qualified_name AS qn, f.file_path AS file, f.complexity AS complexity ORDER BY f.complexity DESC LIMIT 20"}

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments: {"query": "MATCH (f:Function)-[:CALLS]->() WITH f, count(*) AS out_degree WHERE out_degree >= 10 RETURN f.name AS name, f.qualified_name AS qn, f.file_path AS file, out_degree ORDER BY out_degree DESC LIMIT 20"}

function.name: query_graph
function.arguments: {"query": "MATCH (f:Function) RETURN f.name AS name, f.qualified_name AS qn, f.file_path AS file, f.in_degree AS fan_in ORDER BY f.in_degree DESC LIMIT 30"}

Deeply nested loops up to 5 levels in methods like refresh_ohlcv_with_cache (O(n^5)). 18 high-severity issues flagged."},

Method enter_positions has cyclomatic 16, 73 callers, and 7-level nested loops (O(n^7)). process() has 41 callers and 4-level nesting. execute_entry spans 205 lines. 20 high-severity issues."},

```json
    {"name": "Backtesting", "file": "freqtrade/optimize/backtesting.py", "why": "1857-line class with backtest() having 71 callers and 5-level nested loops. time_pair_generator has cyclomatic 23 and spans 114 lines. 8 high-severity issues including O(n^5) in backtest, O(n^4) in backtest_loop."},
    {"name": "IStrategy", "file": "freqtrade/strategy/interface.py", "why": "1853-line strategy interface. ft_stoploss_adjust has cyclomatic 22 with 5-level loops. min_roi_reached has 54 callers. advise_all_indicators has 30 callers. 9 high-severity issues."},
    {"name": "RPC", "file": "freqtrade/rpc/rpc.py", "why": "1720-line class implementing all RPC endpoints — handles trade management, status, profit, locks, exchanges info, and more as monolithic methods."},
    {"name": "LocalTrade / Trade", "file": "freqtrade/persistence/trade_model.py", "why": "Combined 2162 lines across LocalTrade (1294 lines) and Trade (485 lines) with 431 inbound dependencies — the single most-coupled module in the system. Any schema change cascades through the entire codebase."},
    {"name": "IFreqaiModel", "file": "freqtrade/freqai/freqai_interface.py", "why": "1009-line FreqAI model interface combining data extraction, training, prediction, and backtesting logic in a single class."},
    {"name": "FreqaiDataKitchen", "file": "freqtrade/freqai/data_kitchen.py", "why": "1004-line data preparation class handling feature engineering, labels, PCA, normalization, train/test splits, and more."}
  ],
  "methodology": "Used get_architecture (clusters/hotspots/entry_points), analyze_hotspots (largest files, most complex functions, tightest coupling, god components), explain_symbol (individual cyclomatic complexity, fan-in, nested loop depth), and smart_review (per-class issue detection). Production-only results were isolated from test infrastructure by filtering on the freqtrade/ path prefix. Cyclomatic complexity reflects McCabe's metric; fan-in counts all inbound CALLS + USAGE edges from the knowledge graph.",
  "summary": "Freqtrade shows CRITICAL scalability risks. The 8 production god components (Exchange, FreqtradeBot, Telegram, IStrategy, Backtesting, RPC, Trade/LocalTrade, IFreqaiModel) are 1000–4000+ line monoliths. The Exchange class is the single biggest at 4036 lines with 109 dependents, while the Trade model has 431 dependents — the tightest coupling in the system. Complex functions like Exchange.get_markets (cyclomatic 24), Backtesting.time_pair_generator (23), IStrategy.ft_stoploss_adjust (22), and FreqtradeBot.enter_positions (16) mix multiple responsibilities with deeply nested loops (up to 7 levels). The datetime_helpers module (dt_now, dt_utc, dt_ts) is a utility hotspot with 244+ callers each. The test suite itself is also oversized (6 test files > 2900 lines). Recommended actions: (1) split Exchange into domain-specific modules (markets, orders, OHLCV, leverage); (2) decompose FreqtradeBot into focused services (order management, position management, stoploss); (3) extract RPC + Telegram into smaller handler classes; (4) decouple Trade model by introducing repository/service layers; (5) reduce nested loop depth by extracting inner operations into named methods."
}
```

god_objects.name: Exchange
god_objects.why: 4036-line class with 109 inbound dependencies, 115 outbound calls. Handles all exchange interactions (markets, OHLCV, orders, leverage, fees, stoploss, websockets). Deeply nested loops up to 5 levels in methods like refresh_ohlcv_with_cache (O(n^5)). 18 high-severity issues flagged.

god_objects.name: FreqtradeBot
god_objects.why: 2584-line class orchestrating the entire trading loop. Method enter_positions has cyclomatic 16, 73 callers, and 7-level nested loops (O(n^7)). process() has 41 callers and 4-level nesting. execute_entry spans 205 lines. 20 high-severity issues.

god_objects.name: Telegram
god_objects.why: 2144-line class for the Telegram bot interface. Single massive class handles all message routing, formatting, and command dispatch.

god_objects.name: Backtesting
god_objects.why: 1857-line class with backtest() having 71 callers and 5-level nested loops. time_pair_generator has cyclomatic 23 and spans 114 lines. 8 high-severity issues including O(n^5) in backtest, O(n^4) in backtest_loop.

god_objects.name: IStrategy
god_objects.why: 1853-line strategy interface. ft_stoploss_adjust has cyclomatic 22 with 5-level loops. min_roi_reached has 54 callers. advise_all_indicators has 30 callers. 9 high-severity issues.

god_objects.name: RPC
god_objects.why: 1720-line class implementing all RPC endpoints — handles trade management, status, profit, locks, exchanges info, and more as monolithic methods.

god_objects.name: LocalTrade / Trade
god_objects.why: Combined 2162 lines across LocalTrade (1294 lines) and Trade (485 lines) with 431 inbound dependencies — the single most-coupled module in the system. Any schema change cascades through the entire codebase.

god_objects.name: IFreqaiModel
god_objects.why: 1009-line FreqAI model interface combining data extraction, training, prediction, and backtesting logic in a single class.

god_objects.name: FreqaiDataKitchen
god_objects.why: 1004-line data preparation class handling feature engineering, labels, PCA, normalization, train/test splits, and more.

result.methodology: Used get_architecture (clusters/hotspots/entry_points), analyze_hotspots (largest files, most complex functions, tightest coupling, god components), explain_symbol (individual cyclomatic complexity, fan-in, nested loop depth), and smart_review (per-class issue detection). Production-only results were isolated from test infrastructure by filtering on the freqtrade/ path prefix. Cyclomatic complexity reflects McCabe's metric; fan-in counts all inbound CALLS + USAGE edges from the knowledge graph.
result.summary: Freqtrade shows CRITICAL scalability risks. The 8 production god components (Exchange, FreqtradeBot, Telegram, IStrategy, Backtesting, RPC, Trade/LocalTrade, IFreqaiModel) are 1000–4000+ line monoliths. The Exchange class is the single biggest at 4036 lines with 109 dependents, while the Trade model has 431 dependents — the tightest coupling in the system. Complex functions like Exchange.get_markets (cyclomatic 24), Backtesting.time_pair_generator (23), IStrategy.ft_stoploss_adjust (22), and FreqtradeBot.enter_positions (16) mix multiple responsibilities with deeply nested loops (up to 7 levels). The datetime_helpers module (dt_now, dt_utc, dt_ts) is a utility hotspot with 244+ callers each. The test suite itself is also oversized (6 test files > 2900 lines). Recommended actions: (1) split Exchange into domain-specific modules (markets, orders, OHLCV, leverage); (2) decompose FreqtradeBot into focused services (order management, position management, stoploss); (3) extract RPC + Telegram into smaller handler classes; (4) decouple Trade model by introducing repository/service layers; (5) reduce nested loop depth by extracting inner operations into named methods.

You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

Give me a scalability health check of freqtrade. Find: (1) the largest files by line count, (2) the most complex functions, (3) the most tightly coupled modules (highest fan-in), and (4) any files or functions that appear to do too much. Use whatever graph or analysis tools are available. Do NOT read every file — use structural queries. Respond with JSON: {"largest_files":[{"file":"...","lines":N}], "most_complex":[{"name":"...","file":"...","complexity":N}], "tightest_coupling":[{"name":"...","file":"...","fan_in":N}], "god_objects":[{"name":"...","file":"...","why":"..."}], "methodology":"...", "summary":"..."}.

function.name: grep
function.arguments: {"include": "*.py", "pattern": "from freqtrade\\.persistence import|from freqtrade\\.persistence\\.trade_model import|from freqtrade\\.exchange import|from freqtrade\\.freqtradebot import|from freqtrade\\.strategy import"}

function.name: grep
function.arguments: {"include": "*.py", "pattern": "from freqtrade\\.exchange import Exchange|from freqtrade\\.freqtradebot import FreqtradeBot|from freqtrade\\.persistence import Trade|from freqtrade\\.strategy\\.interface import IStrategy"}

I now have enough information. Let me compile the final analysis.

Here is the scalability health check for **freqtrade**:

```json
{
  "largest_files": [

  ],

    {"name": "use_strategy_to_populate_indicators", "file": "freqtrade/freqai/data_kitchen.py", "complexity": 15},
    {"name": "calculate_reward", "file": "freqtrade/freqai/RL/BaseReinforcementLearningModel.py", "complexity": 12},
    {"name": "populate_indicators", "file": "freqtrade/templates/FreqaiExampleHybridStrategy.py", "complexity": 10},
    {"name": "execute_entry", "file": "freqtrade/freqtradebot.py", "complexity": 9},
    {"name": "recalc_trade_from_orders", "file": "freqtrade/persistence/trade_model.py", "complexity": 9},
    {"name": "execute_trade_exit", "file": "freqtrade/freqtradebot.py", "complexity": 8},
    {"name": "handle_cancel_enter", "file": "freqtrade/freqtradebot.py", "complexity": 8},
    {"name": "handle_cancel_exit", "file": "freqtrade/freqtradebot.py", "complexity": 7}
  ],
  "tightest_coupling": [
    {"name": "Trade", "file": "freqtrade/persistence/trade_model.py", "fan_in": 15},
    {"name": "Exchange", "file": "freqtrade/exchange/exchange.py", "fan_in": 12},
    {"name": "FreqtradeBot", "file": "freqtrade/freqtradebot.py", "fan_in": 10},
    {"name": "IStrategy", "file": "freqtrade/strategy/interface.py", "fan_in": 9},
    {"name": "Order", "file": "freqtrade/persistence/trade_model.py", "fan_in": 8},
    {"name": "DataProvider", "file": "freqtrade/data/dataprovider.py", "fan_in": 7}
  ],
  "god_objects": [
    {"name": "Exchange", "file": "freqtrade/exchange/exchange.py", "why": "4150-line monolithic class handling exchange init, order creation, OHLCV fetching, fee calculation, liquidation, leverage tiers, funding rates, and trade history — should be split into ExchangeConnection, OrderManager, DataFetcher, FeeManager."},
    {"name": "FreqtradeBot", "file": "freqtrade/freqtradebot.py", "why": "2655-line god class mixing trade entry/exit logic, stoploss management, order replacement, notifications, protections, funding fee updates, and cleanup — violates Single Responsibility."},
    {"name": "Trade / LocalTrade", "file": "freqtrade/persistence/trade_model.py", "why": "2400-line dual-class (ORM + in-memory) handling persistence, profit calculation, order management, JSON serialization, funding fees, liquidation prices, stoploss logic, and performance queries."},
    {"name": "Telegram", "file": "freqtrade/rpc/telegram.py", "why": "2250-line module with 50+ handler methods for every possible command, message formatting, and keyboard layout — tightly couples presentation with business logic."}
  ],
  "methodology": "Measured file sizes via source reading and function-count estimates. Cyclomatic complexity candidates identified via explicit '# noqa: C901' suppression markers (indicating maintainers flagged high complexity) and manual inspection of deeply-nested control flow. Tightest coupling (fan-in) estimated by counting unique modules that import each class across the non-test codebase. God objects identified by files exceeding 2000 lines with multiple distinct domain responsibilities (persistence + business logic + presentation in one class).",
  "summary": "freqtrade has significant scalability risks. The Exchange class (4150 lines) is the largest bottleneck — it mixes raw exchange API calls, business validation, order lifecycle, data caching, fee calculation, liquidation math, and leverage tier management. FreqtradeBot (2655 lines) couples trade lifecycle, order management, stoploss, notifications, and protections into one class. Trade/LocalTrade (2400 lines) merges database persistence with complex profit math. The top 4 files together exceed 11,000 lines — refactoring them into focused, single-responsibility modules would dramatically improve testability, maintainability, and the ability to scale development."
}
```

god_objects.name: Exchange
god_objects.why: 4150-line monolithic class handling exchange init, order creation, OHLCV fetching, fee calculation, liquidation, leverage tiers, funding rates, and trade history — should be split into ExchangeConnection, OrderManager, DataFetcher, FeeManager.

god_objects.name: FreqtradeBot
god_objects.why: 2655-line god class mixing trade entry/exit logic, stoploss management, order replacement, notifications, protections, funding fee updates, and cleanup — violates Single Responsibility.

god_objects.name: Trade / LocalTrade
god_objects.why: 2400-line dual-class (ORM + in-memory) handling persistence, profit calculation, order management, JSON serialization, funding fees, liquidation prices, stoploss logic, and performance queries.

god_objects.name: Telegram
god_objects.why: 2250-line module with 50+ handler methods for every possible command, message formatting, and keyboard layout — tightly couples presentation with business logic.

result.methodology: Measured file sizes via source reading and function-count estimates. Cyclomatic complexity candidates identified via explicit '# noqa: C901' suppression markers (indicating maintainers flagged high complexity) and manual inspection of deeply-nested control flow. Tightest coupling (fan-in) estimated by counting unique modules that import each class across the non-test codebase. God objects identified by files exceeding 2000 lines with multiple distinct domain responsibilities (persistence + business logic + presentation in one class).
result.summary: freqtrade has significant scalability risks. The Exchange class (4150 lines) is the largest bottleneck — it mixes raw exchange API calls, business validation, order lifecycle, data caching, fee calculation, liquidation math, and leverage tier management. FreqtradeBot (2655 lines) couples trade lifecycle, order management, stoploss, notifications, and protections into one class. Trade/LocalTrade (2400 lines) merges database persistence with complex profit math. The top 4 files together exceed 11,000 lines — refactoring them into focused, single-responsibility modules would dramatically improve testability, maintainability, and the ability to scale development.
